body:
Page 1 of 2 
Bylaw No. 14773 
 
CITY OF BURNABY 
 
BYLAW NO. 14773 
 
 
A BYLAW providing for the 
 
borrowing of moneys to meet 
 
the current lawful expenditures of the City  
 
WHEREAS Council is empowered by section 177 of the Community Charter to 
provide by bylaw for the borrowing of money that may be necessary to meet current lawful 
expenditures; 
 
AND WHEREAS Council deems it desirable and in the public interest to borrow 
the sum of $20,000,000 to meet current lawful expenditures of the City from January 1, 2026 to 
December 31, 2026; and the said sum does not exceed the amount of borrowing that may be 
authorized pursuant to the said section 177; 
 
NOW THEREFORE the Council of the City of Burnaby ENACTS as follows: 
 
1. 
This Bylaw may be cited as BURNABY TEMPORARY FINANCING BYLAW 
2026. 
 
2. 
The Council is hereby authorized and empowered to borrow upon the credit of the 
City from any person or persons, body or bodies corporate, a sum or sums not exceeding Twenty 
Million Dollars ($20,000,000) during the period January 1 to December 31, 2026 in such amounts 
and at such times as the same may be required. 
 
3. 
All the moneys so borrowed during the period January 1 to December 31, 2026 and 
interest payable thereon shall be repaid on or before the 31st day of December, 2026. 
 
 

Page 2 of 2 
Bylaw No. 14773 
 
 
4. 
Forms of obligation or obligations may be given as a requirement or 
acknowledgment of the liability or liabilities incurred pursuant to this bylaw.  Those forms may 
be promissory or demand notes and/or such other supporting documentation as may be required 
by the lender and shall be sealed with the corporate seal and signed by the Mayor or in his absence 
the Acting Mayor and countersigned by the Chief Financial Officer or the Chief Administrative 
Officer. 
 
Read a first time this 28th day of October, 2025 
 
Read a second time this 28th day of October, 2025 
Read a third time this 28th day of October, 2025 
Reconsidered and adopted this 25th day of November, 2025 
 
 
 
MAYOR 
 
 
                   DEPUTY CORPORATE OFFICER 
Signed Original Bylaw on file.

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby authorizes $20M temporary borrowing for 2026 city operations
minutes_briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has passed Bylaw 14773, which allows the city to borrow up to $20 million during 2026 to cover its day-to-day operating expenses. This is a routine financing bylaw that lets the city manage cash flow between January 1 and December 31, 2026. All borrowed money plus interest must be repaid by December 31, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby temporary financing bylaw borrowing period ends
